programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Sharon's husband Chris sees a family tragedy as the perfect opportunity to escape from prison. Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke and Linda Robson

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Heaton is rattled to learn psychopathic arsonist Ray Moore is in the area and warns his wife to get out of London immediately, leaving Mickey and Jack puzzling over what this man means to their superior officer. A terrified youngster flees rather than give evidence against a well-known local thug, but Matt Hinckley proves his worth by getting a conviction when the case comes to court

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: In a special episode focusing on Maddy in the aftermath of her attack, the doctor is faced with the choice of staying at Holby or joining Dan Clifford in Europe. The former consultant bonds with Linden, hoping to persuade Ric to interview her for his new position, while she treats a victim who has been stabbed - an injury that is very close to home. Featuring a guest appearance by Peter Wingfield

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Unable to get through another hard day at work, Simon compensates by taking amphetamines, but his behaviour soon arouses suspicion among his colleagues. When he later vanishes, Lara and Abs are shocked to find him passed out at home. Charlie and Jim clash over the latter's plans for the hospital, and Luke invites Bex out for a drink after she takes pity on him during his suspension

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: A member of the Sken gang is stabbed and Lewis is tempted to bend the rules to investigate the involvement of his cousin Dominic. Meanwhile, a boy is knocked off his bike and killed during a car chase. Will and Diane trace the vehicle involved back to Heaton's old enemy Ray Moore, prompting Jack to dig deeper - only to learn one of Moore's known associates has been reported missing

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Arthur and Willy do battle with the authorities. Roy finally agrees to move in with Pat. Robbie turns vegetarian and Ted and Tony argue over the feasibility of Ted's new venture

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Ricky proves difficult over Roy's plan to move in. Cindy is going to her Mums and sends David a 'Dear John' fax. Arthur avoids a Council PR lady

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: Inverness is shocked by the death of a teacher whose fiance is serving overseas, and Elizabeth realises Henry's relationship with her grandson is not just professional

programme.title: Peak Practice
programme.desc: Alex flees the practice and heads back home to York to drown her sorrows, hotly pursued by Tom, who tries to persuade her to face up to her illness. Back at the surgery, Will struggles to hold the fort as a possible measles epidemic drives worried mothers to extremes over their threatened toddlers. Drama, starring Maggie O'Neill, Simon Shepherd and Gray O'Brien

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: James tries to save a litter of piglets, while Tristan has problems of his own. Rural drama, starring Christopher Timothy and Peter Davison

programme.title: As Time Goes By
programme.desc: Lionel suffers embarrassment when he walks in on Sandy in the bath, and once the sniggers subside Jean decides to have a second bathroom installed and calls in the builders. As the tension increases, she realises a minor incident has been turned into a major issue that she can ill afford

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Richard is forced to think on his feet when he forgets his wedding anniversary and tells Hyacinth he has arranged to have a security alarm fitted as a present. Social-climbing comedy, starring Patricia Routledge and Clive Swift

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth plans an extravagant riverside picnic and invites all and sundry from the higher echelons of society, including the vicar and his wife. Alas for her dreams of social success, the event proves a wash-out. Patricia Routledge stars

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Smiler reminisces about a woman who escaped his advances during the war without leaving her contact details, so Truly, Alvin and Billy volunteer to track her down - even though it means a foray into hostile territory. Long-running comedy series, starring Frank Thornton, Brian Murphy, Keith Clifford and Stephen Lewis

programme.title: Rosemary & Thyme
programme.desc: Laura and Rosemary are called in to investigate when gardens entered for a village contest are attacked by a disease. Further sinister undercurrents are exposed when reigning champion Donald Westward is found dead and recriminations start to fly as buried rivalries come to the surface. Felicity Kendal and Pam Ferris star

programme.title: Strike
programme.desc: Conclusion of the two-part adaptation of JK Rowling's detective novel. Strike and Robin become suspicious of Fancourt after he makes a revealing mistake during a TV interview, while an encounter at a party leads to further suspicious revelations. The authorship of the fatal novel comes under question, and a literary analyst is called in to examine the test. Matthew is less than happy to discover that Strike and Robin are working together again, while Leonora discovers a mysterious purchase was made on the family credit card

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The team investigates claims that celebrity chef Kitty Campbell murdered her husband Bertie during the 1960s. A former assistant of the couple claims Bertie was gay, and that she killed him for fear of a scandal wrecking her career - but Halford thinks they are being taken for a ride. Honor Blackman and Stephen Tompkinson guest star

programme.title: Spooks
programme.desc: The government of Tazbekstan demands the right to execute its enemies on UK soil in return for replenishing the nation's dwindling energy supplies, but Jo and Lucas refuse to turn a blind eye. When the diplomatic effort fails, Section D masterminds a plan to kill the Tazbek trade secretary - and make it appear as if one of his targets is responsible. Espionage drama, starring Richard Armitage and Miranda Raison

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: A member of the Sken gang is stabbed and Lewis is tempted to bend the rules to investigate the involvement of his cousin Dominic. Meanwhile, a boy is knocked off his bike and killed during a car chase. Will and Diane trace the vehicle involved back to Heaton's old enemy Ray Moore, prompting Jack to dig deeper - only to learn one of Moore's known associates has been reported missing

programme.title: Judge John Deed
programme.desc: The maverick High Court Judge's daughter is arrested during a demonstration, presenting him with the challenge of getting her off the hook without falling foul of powerful enemies he has made in his single-minded push to the top of the legal profession. Martin Shaw, Donald Sinden and Caroline Langrishe star

programme.desc: Phil babysits with help from Grant. Ricky is upset when Frank's belongings are removed. Pat receives an unexpected letter and Ted and Roy celebrate their move into the Square

Meanwhile, Tom and two other rock climbers are involved in a cliff-face accident with unexpected ramifications, and Will gets a shock to see his son Tony again _ but that's just the start of the surprises. Popular medical drama, starring Simon Shepherd, Gray O'Brien, Maggie O'Neill and Eva Pope

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: Mrs Pumphrey throws a birthday party for her beloved Tricky Woo - with James as guest of honour. Rural drama, starring Christopher Timothy and Margaretta Scott

programme.desc: Jean falls out with Lionel, but the tense atmosphere between them does not prevent a visit from her sister-in-law Penny and husband Stephen. Bob Larbey's comedy about late-blossoming love, starring Judi Dench and Geoffrey Palmer

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth disappoints Richard by buying him a pair of skis for his birthday, intending to strap them to the car in yet another attempt to impress the neighbours. However, the scheme backfires when her rich sister Violet arrives and cuts her down to size

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth attends an exclusive auction, while Daisy and Rose try to cool the ardour of a woman who seems determined to marry Daddy. Comedy, starring Patricia Routledge, Judy Cornwell and Josephine Tewson

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Truly discovers a former suitor of Marina's went off to Australia, and convinces her the emigrant did so to forget about her - and that she should stop seeing Howard out of respect to the other man's memory. Long-running comedy series, starring Frank Thornton, Jean Fergusson and Robert Fyfe

programme.title: An Inspector Calls
programme.desc: Adaptation of JB Priestley's stage play. In 1912, a detective (David Thewlis) arrives in a town where a young woman has committed suicide. Interrupting a wealthy family in the midst of a lavish party, he unravels the events that led to the woman's death - an investigation with devastating repercussions as secrets are exposed and each member of the family realises they have all unwittingly played a part. Also starring Miranda Richardson and Ken Stott

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Jack is approached by an old friend who has discovered a diamond worth millions in the flat of a man who died more than 20 years ago. It transpires the precious stone was stolen in a safety deposit box heist, but the dead man's passport shows he was out of the country when the crime was committed. Delving deeper into his past, the team discovers some well-concealed secrets - including a daughter who's still unaware of her true parentage

programme.title: Spooks
programme.desc: Ros goes undercover at a gathering of the Bendorf Group, made up of eight of the world's richest men. When armed terrorists take them all hostage and put one of the members on trial live on the internet, she is locked in a booby-trapped bunker with the rest of the captives and forced to seek help from an unlikely source. Espionage drama, starring Hermione Norris and Peter Firth, with Antonia Campbell-Hughes

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: John Heaton reveals that hit-and-run driver Paddy Morgan has disappeared and may have been killed by Ray Moore's hit-man. programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: The Sun Hill relief are walking on eggshells when Gina's married lover goes missing and his wife turns up at the station with a ransom note from the kidnapper. Despite his injuries, the youngster hasn't lost his appetite for mixing it with the local heavies

programme.desc: Roy is given a real East End welcome when Pat throws a party to herald his arrival in the Square. Ted is in for a surprise when Sarah shows him how she spent her afternoon

Meanwhile, Will wonders how many more revelations about his son he can take as the news that he's soon to become a grandad slowly sinks in. Rural medical drama, starring Simon Shepherd and Gray O'Brien

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: James comes to the aid of an isolated family that has not ventured away from home since 1929. Veterinary drama, starring Christopher Timothy and Robert Hardy

programme.desc: Hyacinth cannot suppress her excitement when she hears Emmet is rehearsing an amateur production of The Boyfriend, and resolves to land herself a part in the show. Suburban sitcom, starring Patricia Routledge, Clive Swift and David Griffin

programme.desc: Although Emmet's musical is already fully cast, Hyacinth remains convinced that his shyness is the only reason she has not been given a starring role. Suburban sitcom, starring Patricia Routledge, David Griffin and Geoffrey Hughes

programme.title: The Inspector Lynley Mysteries
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. The detective investigates when former war photographer Peter Rooker is found shot dead. It is not long before he uncovers a trail leading from tabloid newspaper baron Eddie Price and editor Melissa Booth to an executed Bosnian family, but when he enlists his wife's help in determining the psychological history behind the murder, a fatal chain of events begins

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Standing loses £10,000 in a high-stakes poker game - but his opponent, a bookmaker, agrees to write off the debt if the detective can bring his father's killer to justice. Lane helps him uncover a connection with the case of a champion greyhound abducted in the 1980s, and a map comes to light that appears to lead to the missing dog's grave. Meanwhile, a widow mounts a protest outside the station

programme.title: Spooks
programme.desc: Lucas's Russian torturer Oleg Darshavin visits London with information he is willing to share only with his former prisoner. Harry's fears that he retains a psychological hold over the agent appear to be founded when Lucas drops off the radar and invites Darshavin to his own flat - a move that jeopardises the operation when the CIA's Sarah Caulfield calls by during the meeting. Espionage drama, starring Richard Armitage and Peter Firth

programme.desc: Christina Trevanion takes on Serhat Ahmet as they search for items in the antiques shops of North Wales and the Midlands on their way to an auction in Bolton

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Hosted by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: The UK's weather is becoming more extreme, with unprecedented rainfall, sunshine and sustained high temperatures during 2020. In this programme, Justin Rowlatt visits communities around Britain battered by this year's extreme weather to find out how they have coped. With access to Met Office data and experts explaining how hot and wet every part of the UK could become, he discovers a future of more heatwaves, intense storms and little snow for most of the nation, and asks whether Britain is ready for the even wilder weather that is coming

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Sharon returns to the pub to find Ian lying motionless on the floor and immediately calls Kathy. Unable to find a pulse, they panic as they wait for the ambulance to rush him to hospital, while the police are quickly on the scene and suspicions are running high. With so many locals having an axe to grind, who would want Ian dead?

programme.title: The Vicar of Dibley
programme.desc: Geraldine is keen to find a celebrity to open the autumn fair in style - and Alice inadvertently comes up with the ideal candidate by letting slip she is related to one Reg Dwight - AKA Elton John. Comedy, starring Dawn French, and Emma Chambers with a guest appearance by Kylie Minogue

programme.title: The Vicar of Dibley
programme.desc: The Rev Geraldine Granger delivers online sermons for her parishioners during the pandemic, beginning by explaining the difference between metres and miles when it comes to social distancing. Richard Curtis's comedy returns for three mini-episodes, starring Dawn French

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Rob Brydon hosts a festive edition of the comedy panel show from 2016, in which team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack are joined by Richard Osman, Tom Courtenay, Sara Pascoe and Chris Kamara. As usual, all participants will be hoping to deceive one another with plausible lies, such as Richard stating he has never seen Breaking Bad because of his Homes Under the Hammer addiction, Sir Tom claiming he enjoys whiskey on his Christmas morning cornflakes, and Chris explaining that his full name is actually Christmas Kamara. The team also presents a somewhat unusual take on the Nativity

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Christmas is supposed to be a time for family and friends, so when it seems that this year Mrs Brown cannot please anyone, she wonders if it would have been better had she never been born. Enter Clyde, a mysterious figure claiming to be an angel, who tells the doting mother he can show her what life would have been like without her - and before she knows it, she finds herself in a world she doesn't recognise, and that doesn't recognise her. Brendan O'Carroll stars in this 2019 spoof of classic Christmas movie It's a Wonderful Life, with former Corrie star Kevin Kennedy (Curly Watts)

programme.title: Angels of the North
programme.desc: There is a surprise departure from the staff, but there is also news of a new arrival at the salon

programme.title: Angels of the North
programme.desc: It is a big week for one of the team as she gets a long-awaited promotion, and also goes on her first-ever date, which does not turn out how she expected

programme.title: Angels of the North
programme.desc: There is a break-in at the salon so Sammyjo and the girls turn detective, while Kallie's smelly staffroom habits become too much for the team

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong hosts an extended edition of the satirical quiz, with Hannah Fry and Phil Wang joining Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's events

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host greets actor and writer Stephen Fry, who is promoting Troy, his latest reworking of the Greek myths and Mamma Mia star Amanda Seyfried discusses new David Fincher film Mank. Back to the Future star and Parkinson's disease activist Michael J Fox chats about his memoir No Time Like the Future: An Optimist Considers Mortality, Nina Sosanya talks about her new Christmas drama, TV chef Jamie Oliver explains how his new book 7 Ways manages to breathe new life into staple ingredients and comedian Tom Allen talks about his memoir No Shame. Plus, Dolly Parton makes a special appearance performing the seaonal staple I Saw Mommy Kissing Santa Claus

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Paralympic great Hannah Cockcroft, Great Britain sprinter Harry Aikines-Aryeetey, Olympic hockey champion Kate Richardson-Walsh and Toronto Wolfpack forward Jon Wilkin join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker for the quiz

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Sally Nugent present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell visit a Victorian semi in London's Forest Hill, a terraced house in Stoke-on-Trent, and a piece of land in Staffordshire

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: A dog owner displays very bad behaviour when it comes to their pet pooch's insurance policy and a fraudster tries to cash in on his holiday insurance with forged document

programme.title: Saved on Camera
programme.desc: Footage shows the moment a teenager fell from a waterfall, and on a busy dual carriageway, motorists attempt to rescue a man who has passed out at the wheel of his van

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Charles Hanson and Danny Sebastian help two teams in Brackley, Northamptonshire, while presenter Natasha Raskin Sharp finds out more about life at Combe Mill, Oxfordshire

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Karen sees a new side to Tom, Daniel and Bear interview nursing candidates, Valerie offers Lily support, and Sid relieves an influencer from the pressures of social media

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: A couple with a passion for dogs and horses seek a dream country home in Norfolk for £850,000, Sonali Shah tries to find them a property with enough land for their animals

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore is in Surrey checking out the cast-offs at Witley Recycling Centre, which this time are an old chest of drawers, a pair of chairs and a woollen blanket

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Porcelain dealer Serhat Ahmet and jewellery expert Christina Trevanion play to their strengths in the antiques shops of the West Midlands. Plus, an insight to Tudor cleanliness

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious answers. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones presents another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Jack leads the case as the police investigate Ian's attack but with so many grudges against him, it's not proving easy. However, it is not long before they get a breakthrough and make an arrest. Kat and Kush make plans for a new life away from the Square

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Chloe and Cameron are driven further apart when they end up competing for the same job - and tension rises even further when Cameron deliberately sabotages his rival's chances. Skyler, Jeong and Josh work together as a team to save a gravely ill patient, under Henrik's watchful eye. Josh begins to see there is more to Skyler than he gave her credit for. Sacha and Jodie band together after their explosive domestic outburst

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: With Christmas approaching, the Harpers spend a day shopping and getting into the festive spirit, but their joy proves short-lived when their tube train suddenly grinds to a halt and they face the prospect of spending the holiday on the Underground. Christmas special of the sitcom from 2003, with a guest appearance by Richard Whiteley

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: The contest reaches the semi-finals, in which the remaining 10 chefs must prepare a dish based on their favourite food memory. Five of the chefs will be chosen to go through to the next round, while the remaining five must face an invention test to preserve their place in the contest. This challenge involves preparing a dish for a fine dining food delivery experience in 90 minutes

programme.title: We Are Who We Are
programme.desc: Caitlin becomes more daring as she continues to experiment with her sexual identity. Meanwhile, Fraser strikes up a connection with Jonathan

programme.title: We Are Who We Are
programme.desc: Richard tries to re-establish authority over his daughter. He takes her out on a hunting weekend, where he hopes their bond will be mended. Meanwhile, Jonathan and Fraser spend a day off-base together

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Lucy Alexander, Dion Dublin and Martin Roberts explore properties about to be auctioned in Leamington Spa, Plumstead and the Isle of Sheppey

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: Secret filming is the undoing of one man's claim for more than £300,000, when it reveals that he can do everything he claims he can't and there's 'paws for thought' as a pet owner refuses to believe her furry friend isn't covered by a pet insurance policy. A cheeky claimant involved a minor shunt unsuccessfully tries to cash in with a big claim, a mobile phone scammer puts on a convincing performance, and a bus passenger spots an opportunity to line his pockets after a mishap between a bus and a wheelie bin. Presented by Naga Munchetty

programme.title: Saved on Camera
programme.desc: Two friends are rescued from shark infested seas, and petrol station workers in Birmingham save a woman from car jackers. Narrated by Kevin Duala

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles hosts the show from Exeter's Westpoint Arena and tries his hand at basket-weaving on the Somerset levels. With experts Nick Hall and Richard Madley

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Al and Lily receive some bad news, and Rob and Karen look for help for Ella and Tom. Meanwhile, Ruhma advises a husband how to better empathise with his pregnant wife

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps young newlyweds who have a budget of £800,000 and want to move from London to the peace of the Cambridgeshire countryside

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team are in Bradford, where they restore an old lawnmower and an antique cabinet, and find a creative new use for a cable drum

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Antiques experts Christina Trevanion and Serhat Ahmet go out of their comfort zones in their search for old treasures, hitting the shops in Sheffield and Coventry

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Magazine show, with stories of interest from around the UK, plus celebrity guests in the studio, presented by Alex Jones

programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: Granville encourages Gastric to become a fearless hunter in an effort to impress Madge and get closer to her. Mavis senses Mrs Featherstone, aka the Black Widow, making a move on Granville and decides to stand up for herself for once. Mr Newbold goes to the library in the hope of meeting up with Mrs Rossi, while Eric and Cyril finally get their wives to go camping - but who is going to help with the washing-up? David Jason, Tim Healy, Johnny Vegas and Maggie Ollerenshaw star

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team of experts bring four treasured family heirlooms back to life. First up, four experts pool their talents to tackle the repair of a battered old ventriloquist's dummy, before silversmith Brenton West takes on the restoration of a silver running trophy that survived a direct hit during the Blitz in the Second World War. Elsewhere, the experts turn their attention to a vintage portable typewriter and an old shove ha'penny board

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: With just eight contestants remaining in the competition, the first four chefs come together and face-off over two challenges in the hope of making it to the next round. First, the chefs are given a world class demonstration from the celebrated Michelin-starred chef Aktar Islam, before presenting a dish of their own creating for Aktar and the three judges. In the second challenge, the chefs have 90 minutes to prepare two courses of their own design, hoping to secure their place in Finals Week

programme.title: MOBO Awards 2020
programme.desc: Coverage of the 2020 MOBO Awards, hosted by Maya Jama and Chunkz, and featuring performances by Headie One and M Huncho, Ms Banks, Tiwa Savage and Kojey Radical

programme.title: Step into the Ring
programme.desc: Sam, who has Down's syndrome, prepares for his debut in a public wrestling match. Marcus is enjoying success in the ring, and is pursuing romance outside it

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles steps back in time to the 'Georgian days', revisiting the show's episodes in which six teams bought some of the best Georgian items. Eric takes a look at how the items sold at auction, and shares some top tips on what viewers should look out for when scouring the fairs

programme.title: Mountain Vets
programme.desc: Newly qualified vet Patrick is called out to help a farmer whose cow has been struggling all night with a difficult labour. Patrick discovers her calf is breached, and has to reposition it. However, once it's in the correct position, a shock discovery makes his job twice as precarious. Back in Castlewellan, a farmer arrives with a two-week-old lamb that's developed a limp. Some antibiotics and a dose of anti-inflammatories will help the lamb back on its feet. However, vet Maeve notices its eyes are milky white and she has to use all her expertise and knowledge to save the lamb's sight

programme.title: Mary Berry's Simple Comforts
programme.desc: Mary explores how families use food to create their own sense of home, including a father and daughter who have taken their home cooking to the streets with their own food van. Later in the programme, Mary makes some of her own family favourites, including a hearty bolognaise bake with a burrata and heritage tomato salad, and a paneer and roast vegetable curry

programme.title: The Week in Parliament
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's proceedings in Parliament, presented by David Cornock

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Head Hunters
programme.desc: Today's Head Hunters are first-timer and civil servant Roisin, three-time Head Hunter Callum, and customer advisor Dubem, who has been on every show this week

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers Home for Christmas
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers create festive dishes with the help of The One Show's Alex Jones, including a turkey salad, figs stuffed with stilton, and a chocolate and chestnut roulade

programme.title: Nigel Slater's Simple Suppers
programme.desc: The cook demonstrates how to create meals using Christmas dinner leftovers, ensuring nothing bought during the most expensive food shopping trip of the year needs go to waste. He also gets his garden ready for a new year of fruit and vegetable growing, before visiting local allotments and the kitchen plot at Chatsworth House in Derbyshire to show how to make the most of seasonal produce

programme.title: The Belles of St Trinian's (1954)
programme.desc: The mischievous schoolgirls hope to get rich quick by betting on a racehorse belonging to a classmate's wealthy father. However, they first have to foil a gang of thieves planning to steal the animal and outwit an undercover policewoman sent to infiltrate the school disguised as a teacher. Comedy, starring Alastair Sim, Joyce Grenfell and George Cole

programme.title: David Attenborough's Natural Curiosities
programme.desc: David Attenborough examines how fleas are able to jump great distances for their size and attempts to discover how fast a cheetah can run. He also investigates the surprising preventive and curative 'medicine' developed by certain species, including the hippopotamus, which secretes a red slime to act as a highly efficient sun screen

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Raj Bisram and Anita Manning are at Culzean Castle in South Ayrshire, where they assess items including a selection of golf memorabilia and a trio of Moorcroft ceramics

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Alex Jones, Jessica Knappet, Rob Rinder and Karim Zeroual go test their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Fanzine devoted to the ballroom dancing show, featuring an interview with the latest couple to be voted off, as well as behind-the-scenes footage of rehearsals

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys invites four more contestants to brave the black chair to answer questions on the specialist subjects of freshwater fish of the British Isles, Nick Cave, the history of scouting in the UK, and Lin-Manuel Miranda, before they are tested on their general knowledge

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell hosts as two teams who lost their first-round matches get another chance to stay in the competition and reach the second stage. The contestants must use patience, lateral thinking and sheer inspiration to make connections between four things that may appear at first not to be linked, with one set of clues asking what connects a small drink in Armenia, desire in Japan, achieved victory in South Korea and a bell sound in Vietnam

programme.title: Nigella's Cook, Eat, Repeat
programme.desc: Nigella Lawson's grandmother's recipe for creme caramel provides the inspiration for a simple but elegant dessert of caramel custard. She then rifles through her pasta collection to find the ideal shape to go with her spicy sauce made from Italian 'nduja and cavolo nero, followed by a visit to the gadget cupboard for an electric potato masher to prepare her brown butter colcannon, the perfect match to a dish of black pudding meatballs in tomato sauce.

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: The second round of the student quiz continues, with two more teams of four pitting their wits against one another as they attempt to secure a place in the quarter-finals. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions

programme.title: Hospital
programme.desc: A look at cutting-edge treatments being used by clinicians to fight Covid-19, including a 72-year-old intensive care patient is given an experimental drug in the hope that it will improve his lung function. A woman who needs to recover from the virus quickly to have a life-saving mastectomy is randomly selected to receive convalescent plasma, which is harvested from the blood of those who have already had coronavirus

programme.title: The Noughties
programme.desc: Angela Scanlon is joined by Cherry Healey and Tyger Drew-Honey to take a look at the year's cultural highlights, including the first glimpse of new singing talent Adele on Jools Holland's Later, the Spice Girls reuniting, and Shilpa Shetty on the receiving end of racist abuse from her housemates in Celebrity Big Brother

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis

programme.title: The Schools That Chain Boys
programme.desc: An investigation by BBC News Arabic uncovering systemic child abuse inside Islamic schools in Sudan, finding that boys as young as five are routinely chained, shackled and beaten

programme.title: Thanks for the Memories
programme.desc: Part one of two. Joyce is happy with her life, but everything is turned on its head when an accident requires a blood transfusion and she wakes up with memories that are not hers. Romantic comedy based on a novel by Cecelia Ahern, starring Tom Wlaschiha and Tamzin Merchant

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: The team launches Plant Britain, an ambitious two-year challenge aimed at encouraging everyone in the nation to get planting - no matter where they live or how much space they have. Matt Baker is in Cheshire planting the very first trees in what will become the programmes Plant Britain wood, while Anita Rani is in her hometown of Bradford to help residents of the Canterbury estate to plant trees in their front gardens and at a local primary school

programme.title: My Family, the Holocaust & Me with Robert Rinder
programme.desc: Part one of two. The barrister helps the second and third generations of British Jewish families affected by the Holocaust to retrace their relatives' footsteps and discover the full truth about what happened to them. Robert begins by meeting two British Jewish families who want to investigate what happened to their relatives, as well as exploring his own family's stories on both his mother's and his father's sides

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: The chefs are challenged to fillet and smoke a trout for their Skills Test, and serve the results with celeriac remoulade. Their next task is to make a classic crepe suzette, a dish that requires extreme precision, while in the final round, they must impress judges Marcus Wareing, Gregg Wallace and Monica Galetti with their very own two course signature dishes

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Christina Trevanion takes on Serhat Ahmet as they search for items in the antiques shops of North Wales and the Midlands on their way to an auction in Bolton

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Caroline Hawley and David Harper are in Nottinghamshire to help two teams look for items at the Newark Antiques Fair, while Charlie Ross finds out about an everyday object

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: After 15 years of fostering, Stephen and Lynn Smedley were looking forward to their well-earned retirement in Carbis Bay, Cornwall. However, the dream dramatically changed when their daughter died, leaving them caring for their three grandsons. The boys, still grieving for their mum's untimely death, are forced to share a single bedroom with a triple bunkbed. There is only one bathroom and Lynn is forced to do the ironing in the shed due to lack of space. Enter Nick Knowles and the team. Their plan is to reconfigure and enlarge their home to accommodate three growing boys - with the help of hundreds of volunteers

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Head Hunters
programme.desc: Rob Beckett hosts the quiz in which contestants must choose between working together to combine their knowledge or going it alone to claim the prize for themselves

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers Home for Christmas
programme.desc: Antiques expert Paul Martin joins the duo as they cook meat-free festive recipes, including sage and spinach gnocchi, nut roast with all the trimmings and a hazelnut and pear cake

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: Profiles of British comedy stars of the silver screen, featuring the likes of George Formby, Peter Sellers and the Monty Python team. Narrated by Sylvia Syms

programme.title: Blue Murder at St Trinian's (1957)
programme.desc: The mischievous schoolgirls travel by coach to Europe, accompanied by a wanted jewel thief dressed as a woman, and an undercover police detective. Comedy, starring Lionel Jeffries, Joyce Grenfell, Terry-Thomas, George Cole and Alistair Sim

programme.title: David Attenborough's Natural Curiosities
programme.desc: David Attenborough examines animals that are able to expand their bodies, including anacondas and camels. He also reveals the unusual ways in which flamingoes and blue whales eat, examining the ways feeding patterns have influenced both species' behaviour in similar ways

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: From the Boconnoc estate in the Cornish countryside, antiques experts Jonathan Pratt and Nick Hall uncover a pair of Georgian chairs and a children's pedal fire engine

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Alex Jones, Jessica Knappet, Rob Rinder and Karim Zeroual go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: All the backstage gossip from Strictly Come Dancing, as well as the latest traumas

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Bananarama's Sara Dallin and Keren Woodward go hunting for antiques in Buckinghamshire, joined by experts Charles Hanson and David Harper. Before an auction, Keren goes back in time to extol the dance routines of the Georgian period, and Sara and David to learn how Black Beauty inspired a Victorian horse sanctuary that is still operating to this day

programme.title: Great British Christmas Menu
programme.desc: Guest chefs and past series winners Jason Atherton and Tom Aikens join the judging panel, as the contestants get to work on the fish and mains courses. They prepare a salmon and a turbot cooked in a giant antique copper pan, while the main course is a roast combining 12 different birds. The chefs must then judge each other's efforts in a blind taste test

programme.title: QI XS
programme.desc: A compilation of barely broadcastable outtakes from the panel show, hosted by Sandi Toksvig, with regular panellist Alan Davies

programme.title: Industry
programme.desc: Wyndham is impressed when Yasmin sets up a lucrative new deal with a family friend, and completing the arrangement leads to a thawing of tensions between her and Kenny. Eric sets Harper the task of winning back Felim's business - which only exposes the depth of the division between him and Daria. Robert fears for his future, prompting Clement to make a personal revelation

programme.title: TOTP2
programme.desc: Mark Radcliffe sifts through the Top of the Pops archives to present a selection of festive performances, including songs by Slade, the Pogues, Elton John, Take That, Coldplay and Abba

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events. With Emily Maitlis

programme.title: New Elizabethans with Andrew Marr
programme.desc: Documentary following the rigid, class-obsessed Britain of the 1950s to the more liberal, inclusive society in the latter part of the Queen's reign. Andrew Marr begins by taking in the liberal victories of the 'permissive society' - as well as the ferocious backlash of middle England at the perceived erosion of family values. He also looks at people's attitudes to movie starlet Diana Dors and wannabe movie starlet Ruth Ellis - who achieved fame of a completely different kind

programme.title: Thanks for the Memories
programme.desc: Joyce is happy staying with her father while her boyfriend Conor is in Brussels, but cannot understand why she can suddenly speak Italian. Noticing the unspoken attraction between her and Justin, Joyce's boss at the radio station invites him to an office outing at the karaoke evening in the local pub

programme.title: Royal History's Biggest Fibs with Lucy Worsley
programme.desc: The Regency rea is often regarded as genteel and well-ordered, with beautiful buildings, Jane Austen romance and red-coated officers defeating Napoleon at Waterloo. However, historian Lucy Worsley digs behind the facade of Georgian elegance to reveal the fibs that helped conceal a darker side to the Regency and suppressed rebellion in an age of revolution. She discovers that to quell rebellion, monarchy and government relied on spin, secrets and lies

programme.title: Our Food, Our Family with Michela Chiappa
programme.desc: The Welsh-Italian cook travels to Gower to meet and eat with a little-known community of incomers enjoying life on one of the country's most beautiful coastlines. At the heart of the community are the Blytt-Jordens - three generations of Welsh-Norwegians who are among several such clans settled in the area who have only recently discovered each other. In Michela's company, they rustle up traditional Norwegian delicacies from national pudding tilsorte bondepiker to hot waffles and brown cheese and fermented fish. They also get a chance to chat in their first language

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: Four more professional chefs arrive from across the nation to compete, first taking on the infamous Skills Test and recreating dishes set by Marcus and Monica. The first two chefs try their hand at Marcus's challenge of preparing scallops with a romesco sauce and a marinated tomato salad, before the second pair of competitors prepare and cook lambs' kidneys and serve on toast with a Madeira mustard sauce for Monica. In the second round, the four chefs must impress the judges with two stand out Signature Dishes of their own creation, before two of the competitors are sent home

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Porcelain dealer Serhat Ahmet and jewellery expert Christina Trevanion play to their strengths in the antiques shops of the West Midlands. Plus, an insight to Tudor cleanliness

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell visit a Victorian semi in London's Forest Hill, a terraced house in Stoke-on-Trent, and a piece of land in Staffordshire

programme.title: Powering Britain
programme.desc: Keeley Donovan meets people working at Britain's biggest operational nuclear site at Heysham on the Lancashire coast. Heysham 1 and Heysham 2 power stations make enough electricity to power more than four million homes. Keeley follows engineers as they undertake a huge maintenance programme called an outage. But with Britain in lockdown during the coronavirus pandemic will engineers be able to get one of the reactors back online to provide much-needed energy to the nation?

programme.title: A Special School
programme.desc: Rebecca Jones introduces her class of eight autistic boys, each with their own individual needs, including Ethan - who likes to answer the school's telephone. Fifteen-year-old Luke was born with schizencephaly - a hole in his brain - which has left him with long-term physical health problems. Ysgol y Deri is his lifeline and with voice-generating software, teachers are helping him to express his emotions

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Head Hunters
programme.desc: Contestants include journalism student Charlotte and retired police officer Andrew, along with five-time Head Hunter Dubem, who answer questions on rugby union and religion

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers Home for Christmas
programme.desc: Denise Lewis joins the duo as they create a trio of canapes, porcetta pork loin and a prawn and salmon terrine. The retired athlete also explains how she spends her Christmas. Alongside the recipes and fun there are also fantastic ideas for homemade edible Christmas presents

programme.title: Nigel Slater's Simple Suppers
programme.desc: The cook sets out to prove that healthy food can taste great as he prepares a week's worth of sumptuous dishes from seasonal produce. He also tidies his garden and makes plans for the growing season ahead

programme.title: The Pure Hell of St Trinian's (1960)
programme.desc: The girls burn their school to the ground, prompting a mysterious Middle Eastern gentleman to offer new premises for their studies - but he has a sinister ulterior motive for helping the badly behaved youngsters. Comedy, starring Cecil Parker, Joyce Grenfell, George Cole and Irene Handl

programme.title: David Attenborough's Natural Curiosities
programme.desc: David Attenborough explores animals that have developed tool-making skills to help them survive, including orang-utans and crows. He also examines two families with remarkable powers of regenerating unusually large body parts easily - salamanders that do so when a tail or a limb has been bitten off by a predator, and male moose that grow and shed antlers every year

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: A special edition, travelling up and down the county revisiting some of the historic venues from the series, with new valuations and unexpected auctions. Paul Martin tells the incredible story of the Wagoners and their role in the First World War

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Alex Jones, Jessica Knappet, Rob Rinder and Karim Zeroual go test their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Zoe Ball gives all the backstage gossip and exclusive news from Strictly Come Dancing, and Ian Waite gives his thoughts on the couples' training progress

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Loose Women co-hosts Nadia Sawalha and Kaye Adams join forces with experts Tim Medhurst and Anita Manning in the antique shops of Edinburgh and beyond. Nadia picks up quite the menagerie of antique animals, including three monkeys and a reindeer, while Kaye gambles a large chunk of her £400 on an art deco bracelet. An unsavoury incident at the auction and an accusation of foul play makes for a tense finale

programme.title: Great British Christmas Menu
programme.desc: The top three chefs from the fish and main heats cook for the judges, hoping to secure their place as part of the special festive feast in honour of Britain's key workers. For the fish course, there is salmon and turbot, cooked in a giant antique copper pan known as a turbot kettle. For the main course, dishes include venison and a roast that combines 12 different birds. Hosted by Andi Oliver

programme.title: Harlots
programme.desc: Period drama set in London in the 1760s following Margaret Wells as she struggles to reconcile her roles as mother and brothel owner. As Blayne tightens his grip on power, Anne is under pressure, but at what price? North is given valuable information by Lady Leadsom, and Kate attempts to gain the trust of the prince. Starring Lesley Manville and Liv Tyler

programme.title: Amazing Hotels: Life Beyond the Lobby
programme.desc: Giles Coren and Monica Galetti travel more than 120 miles north of the Arctic Circle into Swedish Lapland to work in Icehotel in the village of Jukkasjarvi. Using two-ton blocks of ice hacked from the nearby River Torne, Giles works with a team of international artists to create individually crafted rooms

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig hosts an extended version of the quiz in which Jason Manford, Romesh Ranganathan and Holly Walsh join regular participant Alan Davies to vie for points awarded for interesting - if not always correct - answers. In a Christmas-themed edition, the panellists learn the best way to wrap an awkwardly shaped present and get wine stains out of a carpet

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Emily Maitlis

programme.title: Hospital
programme.desc: A bed shortage in Barnet hospital highlights the perpetual challenge of trying to discharge older patients, alongside the added complexities of Covid-19 infection risks. As autumn begins, the hospital's emergency department is seeing a resurgence of patients after a summer of low attendances, with more people arriving in A&E than almost any other day since last winter, and they are admitting more patients than they are discharging

programme.title: Hospital
programme.desc: Documentary following the day-to-day realities the NHS faces in unprecedented time, focusing on the issues and challenges that continually dominate the headlines

programme.title: Saving Britain's Pubs with Tom Kerridge
programme.desc: For the past 20 years, pubs have been closing down at an alarming rate. Concerned by a crisis in the industry he loves, Tom Kerridge sets out on a mission to revive struggling pubs. He begins at the White Hart in Chilsworthy, Cornwall, where a 300-year-old freehouse, the only pub in the village, is in danger of closing

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: The third lot of quarter-finalists are challenged to create a dish that makes great use of citrus, presented with an array of fruits from kumquats to lemons, as well as other ingredients. They then have to cook two courses for restaurant critics Jay Rayner and Tracey MacLeod, who scrutinise the dishes in each and every detail. However, judges Monica Galetti and Marcus Wareing will ultimately decide who deserves a place in the next round

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Bob Ross: The Happy Painter
programme.desc: Documentary taking a behind-the-scenes look at American painter Bob Ross's journey from humble beginnings to pop culture icon, charting how one local TV commercial evolved into a viral phenomenon that continues to inspire millions around the world. The film reveals the public and private sides of Bob Ross through accounts from close friends and family, childhood photographs and rare archive footage. Interviewees recount his gentle, mild-mannered demeanour and unwavering dedication to wildlife as well as disclosing little-known facts about his famous curly hair - which was naturally straight - and his fascination with fast cars

programme.title: Christmas University Challenge
programme.desc: Teams of distinguished graduates compete on behalf of their former universities in the academic quiz's festive incarnation, with the opening first-round match seeing the University of Leeds taking on Clare College, Cambridge. Among those facing questions from host Jeremy Paxman are broadcaster the Rev Richard Coles and photographer Tim Allen for Leeds, while the Clare team includes writer Marcel Theroux and soprano Elin Manahan Thomas

programme.title: The Art Mysteries with Waldemar Januszczak
programme.desc: The art critic examines some of the art world's biggest enigmas, beginning with Van Gogh's Self-Portrait with Bandaged Ear, asking why the painter cut off his ear

programme.title: Mary Beard's Shock of the Nude
programme.desc: Part two of two. The classicist looks at an eclectic range of works to show how artists have challenged the idea of 'the body beautiful', and explores how artistic nudes can provoke viewers to consider fundamental questions about being human. Mary reflects on Marc Quinn's sculpture of artist Alison Lapper, Sally Mann's images of her own children in the 1990s and the controversy that surrounded their exhibition, and examines society's shifting perception of gender, from the binary of male and female to a more fluid concept

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: Documentary following the story of a joint venture between the Red Army and Pittsburgh Penguins ice hockey teams shortly after the collapse of the Soviet Union. Eccentric marketing whiz Steven Warshaw was sent to Russia and given the task of transforming the joint teams into the greatest show in Moscow at a pivotal moment in US-Russia relations, when oligarchs made their fortunes and multiple murders went unsolved

programme.title: Timeshift
programme.desc: Stella Rimington examines British perceptions of Russia over the past 150 years. The former director general of MI5 journeys to the East End of London in search of Russian revolutionaries and Chopwell, a town once known for its mining that was dubbed 'Little Moscow'. Rimington also talks to former Soviet dissident Vladimir Bukovsky and ex-British ambassador Rodric Braithwaite

programme.title: Russia 1917: Countdown to Revolution
programme.desc: A look at Russia's transition from tsarist autocracy to communist state, focusing on the involvement of three men - Lenin, Trotsky and Stalin, all of whom emerged from obscurity to forge an entirely new political system in the space of a few months. This drama-documentary examines the 245 days that brought these men to supreme power. As the history unfolds writers and historians, including Martin Amis, Orlando Figes, Helen Rappaport, Simon Sebag Montefiore and China Mieville, battle over the meaning of the Russian Revolution and explore how it shaped the world of today. Narrated by Juliet Stevenson

programme.title: Bob Ross: The Happy Painter
programme.desc: Documentary taking a behind-the-scenes look at American painter Bob Ross's journey from humble beginnings to pop culture icon, charting how one local TV commercial evolved into a viral phenomenon that continues to inspire millions around the world. The film reveals the public and private sides of Bob Ross through accounts from close friends and family, childhood photographs and rare archive footage. Interviewees recount his gentle, mild-mannered demeanour and unwavering dedication to wildlife as well as disclosing little-known facts about his famous curly hair - which was naturally straight - and his fascination with fast cars

programme.title: Mary Beard's Shock of the Nude
programme.desc: Part two of two. The classicist looks at an eclectic range of works to show how artists have challenged the idea of 'the body beautiful', and explores how artistic nudes can provoke viewers to consider fundamental questions about being human. Mary reflects on Marc Quinn's sculpture of artist Alison Lapper, Sally Mann's images of her own children in the 1990s and the controversy that surrounded their exhibition, and examines society's shifting perception of gender, from the binary of male and female to a more fluid concept

programme.title: Christmas University Challenge
programme.desc: Jeremy Paxman hosts the second round of the festive quiz featuring teams of famous graduates. This episode pits a team representing Birmingham City University, featuring The Archers' Carole Boyd and gardening expert Bunny Guinness, against alumni of Wadham College, Oxford, including journalists and broadcasters Anne McElvoy and Jonathan Freedland

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross offers tips and advice as demonstrates how to paint a statuesque ridge of peaks overlooking a little country home by the cove

programme.title: The 80s with Dominic Sandbrook
programme.desc: The historian concludes his review by looking at the last few years of the decade and a society transformed by accelerated change. Not only did the late 1980s see the rise of the yuppie and the economic big bang in the City of London, but shopping habits changed with the growing popularity of German cars, French wine, Italian fashion and Scandinavian interior design. Alongside this transformation was a growing disconnection from the political elite, signified by the growing rave culture and the community charge riots. For Margaret Thatcher, widely seen as the architect of much of this change, things would not end well

programme.title: Thatcher: A Very British Revolution
programme.desc: A look at how Margaret Thatcher and her government sought to transform the British economy with far-reaching consequences, while tensions surfaced amongst her closest allies as she tried to improve her public image and events revealed that the Iron Lady had emotional vulnerabilities in relation to her family

programme.title: The Yorkshire Ripper Files: A Very British Crime Story
programme.desc: Documentary examining Peter Sutcliffe's crimes and asking whether 1970s attitudes to women influenced the investigation, leaving the murderer free to kill again. The first episode explores the early years of the investigation from 1975, when the first Ripper murder occurred. The location of the first two murders in Chapeltown, then well-known as Leeds' main red light district, led the police to decide that prostitution was the connection between the attacks. After the second murder in January 1976, the police announced they were hunting a 'prostitute killer' and the investigation became driven by this theory

programme.title: The Yorkshire Ripper Files: A Very British Crime Story
programme.desc: The second part of the documentary examines how promising lines in the Yorkshire Ripper inquiry were overlooked as a hoax tape took centre stage

programme.title: The Valhalla Murders
programme.desc: The team edges nearer to identifying a suspect with the help of a fingerprint lifted from Thor's photograph and CCTV footage from the shopping mall, but this success is jeopardised by a leak to social media that risks putting further lives in danger. In Icelandic

programme.title: The Valhalla Murders
programme.desc: Kata is suspended from duty for losing her gun, while Arnar questions the hospitalised Steinthor, who strenuously denies killing his friend Tomas. Kata gets an intriguing phone call from Hakon, who has discovered something in the basement at Valhalla. In Icelandic

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross offers tips and advice as demonstrates how to paint a statuesque ridge of peaks overlooking a little country home by the cove

programme.title: Thatcher: A Very British Revolution
programme.desc: A look at how Margaret Thatcher and her government sought to transform the British economy with far-reaching consequences, while tensions surfaced amongst her closest allies as she tried to improve her public image and events revealed that the Iron Lady had emotional vulnerabilities in relation to her family

programme.title: Christmas University Challenge
programme.desc: Jeremy Paxman hosts the third round of the festive quiz featuring teams of famous graduates. This episode sees Actors Monica Dolan and Bob Barrett, representing Guildhall School of Music and Drama, in a festive contest with presenter Maryam Moshiri and Arctic explorer Pen Hadow, both former students at UCL

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross offers further advice as he paints a tropical seascape - an exotic ocean masterpiece complete with palm trees swaying in the warm breeze

programme.title: Tudor Monastery Farm
programme.desc: Ruth Goodman, Peter Ginn and Tom Pinfold examine the festive season as it would have been celebrated in 1500 under the reign of Henry VII, concentrating on Christmas Day, New Year's Day and Twelfth Night. They make Tudor decorations, engage in festive revels and prepare feasting delights such as boar's head and shred pies - which were forerunners of mince pies and were made with meat. They also investigate the Tudor origins of Christmas carols, the singing of which was known as wassailing, and learn about the Lord of Misrule - traditionally a commoner placed in authority over his social betters for the festive period and in charge of directing the Christmas revelry

programme.title: Great Barrier Reef
programme.desc: Monty Halls reveals how the Great Barrier Reef is vitally linked to the rest of the planet in many ways. Creatures travel thousands of miles to visit, from tiger sharks, great whales and seabirds to the largest green-turtle gathering on Earth and rarely seen alien-like creatures, such as the tentacled nautilus. The reef is also affected by weather systems travelling from across the Pacific, including cyclones that bring destruction and chaos to the coral and the creatures that live on it

programme.title: The Yorkshire Ripper Files: A Very British Crime Story
programme.desc: The documentary charts the arrest, trial and conviction of Peter Sutcliffe, and the legacy for the relatives of his victims and the survivors of his attacks. The film explores how the trial was jeopardised when witnesses were offered money for exclusives and how the front seats in the court were reserved for VIPs. Going back to survivors of the attacks and relatives of Sutcliffe's victims, the film also explores the legacy left behind by his crimes and finds out what it has been like to live as the child of a Ripper victim

programme.title: The Sound of TV with Neil Brand
programme.desc: The composer addresses the history and use of music in television, beginning by examining the enduring power of the theme tune. On the streets of Coronation Street, he encounters a brass band playing the music that has announced the start of each episode since it began. Following the trail of the soap opera world, Neil meets composer Simon May, creator of the EastEnders theme tune, and travels to the US to talk with Matt Groening, creator of The Simpsons, to uncover how its orchestrated theme music is a homage to classic TV of the past. Themes to Z Cars, Bagpuss, Game of Thrones, Mastermind and Grandstand are also celebrated

programme.title: Make! Craft Britain
programme.desc: The novice crafters discover the ancient art of silver jewellery making, and origami expert Sam Tsang demonstrates how to make a snack box

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross offers further advice as he paints a tropical seascape - an exotic ocean masterpiece complete with palm trees swaying in the warm breeze

Love Monster thinks that since Good Idea Puppy is the guest, she should choose which activity they do first. She decides that they should start with the ice slide. Love Monster and Good Idea Puppy go together and have a great time, but Bad Idea Puppy has to go on his own and struggles to slide down. After the slide, they visit the Frosty Frolics Fayre, and Good Idea Puppy and Love Monster accidentally choose activities made for two all day long. Finally, just as Love Monster and Good Idea Puppy are about to go on a tandem ski through the forest, Bad Idea Puppy asks if this time he can go with his cousin so he won’t be the odd puppy out. Love Monster happily agrees, but soon he realises that frosty frolics aren’t fun on your own, and that Bad Idea Puppy must have been feeling left out all day.

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: The UK's weather is becoming more extreme, with unprecedented rainfall, sunshine and sustained high temperatures during 2020. In this programme, Justin Rowlatt visits communities around Britain battered by this year's extreme weather to find out how they have coped. With access to Met Office data and experts explaining how hot and wet every part of the UK could become, he discovers a future of more heatwaves, intense storms and little snow for most of the nation, and asks whether Britain is ready for the even wilder weather that is coming

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Sharon returns to the pub to find Ian lying motionless on the floor and immediately calls Kathy. Unable to find a pulse, they panic as they wait for the ambulance to rush him to hospital, while the police are quickly on the scene and suspicions are running high. With so many locals having an axe to grind, who would want Ian dead?

programme.title: The Vicar of Dibley
programme.desc: Geraldine is keen to find a celebrity to open the autumn fair in style - and Alice inadvertently comes up with the ideal candidate by letting slip she is related to one Reg Dwight - AKA Elton John. programme.title: The Vicar of Dibley
programme.desc: The Rev Geraldine Granger delivers online sermons for her parishioners during the pandemic, beginning by explaining the difference between metres and miles when it comes to social distancing. Richard Curtis's comedy returns for three mini-episodes, starring Dawn French

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Rob Brydon hosts a festive edition of the comedy panel show from 2016, in which team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack are joined by Richard Osman, Tom Courtenay, Sara Pascoe and Chris Kamara. As usual, all participants will be hoping to deceive one another with plausible lies, such as Richard stating he has never seen Breaking Bad because of his Homes Under the Hammer addiction, Sir Tom claiming he enjoys whiskey on his Christmas morning cornflakes, and Chris explaining that his full name is actually Christmas Kamara. The team also presents a somewhat unusual take on the Nativity

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Christmas is supposed to be a time for family and friends, so when it seems that this year Mrs Brown cannot please anyone, she wonders if it would have been better had she never been born. Enter Clyde, a mysterious figure claiming to be an angel, who tells the doting mother he can show her what life would have been like without her - and before she knows it, she finds herself in a world she doesn't recognise, and that doesn't recognise her. Brendan O'Carroll stars in this 2019 spoof of classic Christmas movie It's a Wonderful Life, with former Corrie star Kevin Kennedy (Curly Watts)

programme.title: Andy's Safari Adventures

programme.title: Magic Hands
programme.desc: Children's programme.

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: he Go Jetters are in snowy Norway to learn about the reindeer that are cared for by the local Sami people. In the spring the reindeer go on a very long journey called migration and as part of this journey they catch a ferry to take them to their warmer summer home. Grandmaster Glitch and the Grimbots are also in Norway, but their Grimbler scares the migrating reindeer and some of them run away from the herd. The Go Jetters need to keep them calm and get them back to the herd so they can finish their migration

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: The UK's weather is becoming more extreme, with unprecedented rainfall, sunshine and sustained high temperatures during 2020. In this programme, Justin Rowlatt visits communities around Britain battered by this year's extreme weather to find out how they have coped. With access to Met Office data and experts explaining how hot and wet every part of the UK could become, he discovers a future of more heatwaves, intense storms and little snow for most of the nation, and asks whether Britain is ready for the even wilder weather that is coming

programme.title: EastEnders

With so many locals having an axe to grind, who would want Ian dead?

programme.desc: Rob Brydon hosts a festive edition of the comedy panel show from 2016, in which team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack are joined by Richard Osman, Tom Courtenay, Sara Pascoe and Chris Kamara. As usual, all participants will be hoping to deceive one another with plausible lies, such as Richard stating he has never seen Breaking Bad because of his Homes Under the Hammer addiction, Sir Tom claiming he enjoys whiskey on his Christmas morning cornflakes, and Chris explaining that his full name is actually Christmas Kamara. The team also presents a somewhat unusual take on the Nativity

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Christmas is supposed to be a time for family and friends, so when it seems that this year Mrs Brown cannot please anyone, she wonders if it would have been better had she never been born. Enter Clyde, a mysterious figure claiming to be an angel, who tells the doting mother he can show her what life would have been like without her - and before she knows it, she finds herself in a world she doesn't recognise, and that doesn't recognise her. Brendan O'Carroll stars in this 2019 spoof of classic Christmas movie It's a Wonderful Life, with former Corrie star Kevin Kennedy (Curly Watts)

programme.title: The Man Who Wanted to Fly (2018)
programme.desc: The story of octogenarian farmer Bobby Coote's efforts to fulfil a lifelong ambition to fly a plane, following him as he cuts out a runway in his neighbour Sean's fields and even builds a hangar in this small rural community in Cavan. Bobby gets no encouragement from his brother Ernie, who thinks the whole thing is daft, but he is determined to take to the skies if it's the last thing he does

programme.desc: Alex Jones presents another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Jack leads the case as the police investigate Ian's attack but with so many grudges against him, it's not proving easy. However, it is not long before they get a breakthrough and make an arrest. Kat and Kush make plans for a new life away from the Square

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Chloe and Cameron are driven further apart when they end up competing for the same job - and tension rises even further when Cameron deliberately sabotages his rival's chances. Skyler, Jeong and Josh work together as a team to save a gravely ill patient, under Henrik's watchful eye. Josh begins to see there is more to Skyler than he gave her credit for. Sacha and Jodie band together after their explosive domestic outburst

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: With Christmas approaching, the Harpers spend a day shopping and getting into the festive spirit, but their joy proves short-lived when their tube train suddenly grinds to a halt and they face the prospect of spending the holiday on the Underground. Christmas special of the sitcom from 2003, with a guest appearance by Richard Whiteley

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: The contest reaches the semi-finals, in which the remaining 10 chefs must prepare a dish based on their favourite food memory. Five of the chefs will be chosen to go through to the next round, while the remaining five must face an invention test to preserve their place in the contest. This challenge involves preparing a dish for a fine dining food delivery experience in 90 minutes

programme.title: Spotlight
programme.desc: Investigations into social and political issues affecting Northern Ireland

programme.title: We Are Who We Are
programme.desc: Caitlin becomes more daring as she continues to experiment with her sexual identity. Meanwhile, Fraser strikes up a connection with Jonathan

programme.title: We Are Who We Are
programme.desc: Richard tries to re-establish authority over his daughter. He takes her out on a hunting weekend, where he hopes their bond will be mended. Meanwhile, Jonathan and Fraser spend a day off-base together

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Lucy Alexander, Dion Dublin and Martin Roberts explore properties about to be auctioned in Leamington Spa, Plumstead and the Isle of Sheppey

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: Secret filming is the undoing of one man's claim for more than £300,000, when it reveals that he can do everything he claims he can't and there's 'paws for thought' as a pet owner refuses to believe her furry friend isn't covered by a pet insurance policy. A cheeky claimant involved a minor shunt unsuccessfully tries to cash in with a big claim, a mobile phone scammer puts on a convincing performance, and a bus passenger spots an opportunity to line his pockets after a mishap between a bus and a wheelie bin. Presented by Naga Munchetty

programme.title: Saved on Camera
programme.desc: Two friends are rescued from shark infested seas, and petrol station workers in Birmingham save a woman from car jackers. Narrated by Kevin Duala

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles hosts the show from Exeter's Westpoint Arena and tries his hand at basket-weaving on the Somerset levels. With experts Nick Hall and Richard Madley

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Al and Lily receive some bad news, and Rob and Karen look for help for Ella and Tom. Meanwhile, Ruhma advises a husband how to better empathise with his pregnant wife

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps young newlyweds who have a budget of £800,000 and want to move from London to the peace of the Cambridgeshire countryside

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team are in Bradford, where they restore an old lawnmower and an antique cabinet, and find a creative new use for a cable drum

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Antiques experts Christina Trevanion and Serhat Ahmet go out of their comfort zones in their search for old treasures, hitting the shops in Sheffield and Coventry

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Magazine show, with stories of interest from around the UK, plus celebrity guests in the studio, presented by Alex Jones

programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: Granville encourages Gastric to become a fearless hunter in an effort to impress Madge and get closer to her. Mavis senses Mrs Featherstone, aka the Black Widow, making a move on Granville and decides to stand up for herself for once. Mr Newbold goes to the library in the hope of meeting up with Mrs Rossi, while Eric and Cyril finally get their wives to go camping - but who is going to help with the washing-up? David Jason, Tim Healy, Johnny Vegas and Maggie Ollerenshaw star

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team of experts bring four treasured family heirlooms back to life. First up, four experts pool their talents to tackle the repair of a battered old ventriloquist's dummy, before silversmith Brenton West takes on the restoration of a silver running trophy that survived a direct hit during the Blitz in the Second World War. Elsewhere, the experts turn their attention to a vintage portable typewriter and an old shove ha'penny board

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: With just eight contestants remaining in the competition, the first four chefs come together and face-off over two challenges in the hope of making it to the next round. First, the chefs are given a world class demonstration from the celebrated Michelin-starred chef Aktar Islam, before presenting a dish of their own creating for Aktar and the three judges. In the second challenge, the chefs have 90 minutes to prepare two courses of their own design, hoping to secure their place in Finals Week

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: Imran Khan won power in Pakistan two years ago with a promise to root out corruption and take on the country's vested interests. So how's it going? Rising food prices and the Covid pandemic have left many Pakistanis feeling worse off, while the anti-corruption drive has become a political battleground. Stephen Sackur speaks to Ishaq Dar who was Pakistan's Finance Minister and a key lieutenant of former Prime Minister Nawaz Sharif. Now both men are trying to rally opposition to Imran Khan, but how much credibility do they have?
